quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, visible from Port Angeles, was named after a Greek sailor who sailed under a Spanish name! His real name was Ioannis Phokas, but he explored the Pacific Northwest in 1592 as Juan de Fuca. The waterway connects the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound and is home to orcas, seals, and sea otters.

short_read.title: Why Talking to Your Plants Actually Helps Them Grow
short_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that plants respond to vibrations and sounds, including human voices. Learn how your garden conversations might be doing more good than you think!

medium_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in the Pacific Northwest
medium_read.teaser: Harbor seals are year-round residents of the Strait of Juan de Fuca, and they're full of surprises. From their underwater songs to their remarkable diving abilities, these charming marine mammals have fascinating stories to tell.

long_read.title: The Great Migration: Hungarian Stories from the Old World to America
long_read.teaser: Between 1880 and 1920, nearly two million Hungarians came to America seeking new opportunities. This heartfelt exploration shares their journeys, traditions they brought with them, and the rich cultural heritage they preserved.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer Fawns Are Being Born Now
port_angeles.description: June is fawning season for Olympic Peninsula black-tailed deer! If you spot a fawn alone, don't worry—mother deer often leave their babies hidden while they forage nearby. The fawns have almost no scent, which helps protect them from predators. You might see them in the early morning or evening around Park View Villas.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while hovering.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right, Barbara! An incredible 1,200 beats per minute—about 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! A hummingbird's tiny heart beats about 1,200 times per minute while hovering.
puzzle_1.wrong_response: These tiny birds are amazing—their hearts beat about 1,200 times per minute! That's what powers their incredible hovering ability.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the fastest heart rate of any bird, and they need it to power their wings which beat 50-80 times per second. Despite their size, they're some of nature's most remarkable athletes.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful, Ildiko! Pillangó is such a beautiful word for such a beautiful creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó'—doesn't it sound lovely? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and csillag means star.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language, not related to most European languages but part of the Finno-Ugric family. Many Hungarian words have a musical, flowing quality like 'pillangó.'

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree can live for over 2,000 years and is found in the Olympic Mountains?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Exactly right! Alaska Yellow Cedar can live over 2,000 years—some of the oldest trees in Olympic National Park are this species. Imagine all the history they've witnessed!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the Alaska Yellow Cedar! Some in the Olympic Mountains are over 2,000 years old—they were already ancient when Vikings explored North America. These remarkable trees are treasures of the Peninsula.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Alaska Yellow Cedar trees grow slowly in harsh mountain conditions, which gives them incredibly dense, rot-resistant wood. Some specimens in the Olympics are among the oldest living trees in the world.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
